_note: Placeholder values pending pipeline run. Sears Holdings filed Chapter 11 in October 2018.

grades.label: Market Position
grades.evidence: Share losses to Amazon, Walmart, Target across most categories. The 10-K acknowledges 'we have experienced declining sales' and accelerating store closures.

grades.label: Moat Type & Strength
grades.evidence: No durable competitive advantage in retail operations. Some value in real-estate portfolio, but management is monetising rather than reinvesting in core.

grades.label: Market Quality
grades.evidence: Department-store category in secular decline. E-commerce share growing >20% per year; foot traffic to malls falling. The 10-K's risk factors list ten variants of the same observation.

grades.label: Leadership Quality
grades.evidence: Eddie Lampert (CEO / largest shareholder) emphasises financial re-engineering and asset spinoffs over merchandising. Capital allocation prioritises buybacks and real-estate carve-outs.

pullquote: "The engine saw a lagging position in a disrupted market with no durable moat and a financial-engineering management. It assigned 61% probability to the 'disaster' bucket. Sears filed for Chapter 11 in October 2018 — four years and ten months into the twelve-year window."
pullquote_attr: From the demo notebook · 2014 vintage holdout
